revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:長者ヶ平古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Chojaganaru Kofun, entrance.jpg|location={{ill|Yonago|qid=Q320964|1=Yonago}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.453083|N|133.44005|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}}}}
'''Chōjaganaru Kofun''' (長者ヶ平古墳, also known as Mukōyama No. 5 Kofun) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Yodoe-chō Fukuoka, Yonago City, Tottori Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukoyama Kofun Cluster]] but is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Chōjaganaru Kofun is situated at the southwestern end of the Mukōyama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. The tumulus was partially excavated in 1901 (Meiji 34), revealing a small stone burial chamber (box-type sarcophagus), and trial excavations were conducted in 1988 (Showa 63){{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992|qq=}}{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. The tumulus has a {{ill|scallop-shaped mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a short rectangular front section and a circular rear section, oriented southwest. Traces of fukiishi (covering stones) and cylindrical haniwa (clay figurines) were found on its surface{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. The burial chamber is a {{ill|katagoshiki|ja|片袖式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/katagoshiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (single-sleeve) horizontal stone chamber located in the circular rear section, measuring 10.3 meters in total length{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. Grave goods included a gilt bronze crown, a gilt bronze ring-pommel sword, and other luxurious items, as well as iron swords and spearheads{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster Pamphlet|qq=}}. The tumulus dates to the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster Pamphlet|qq=}}. Among the 16 tumuli in the Mukōyama Kofun Cluster, 14 are designated as National Historic Sites, but Chōjaganaru Kofun is not{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster Pamphlet|qq=}}. == History == * Edo Period: Grave robbers reportedly removed numerous artifacts, including pottery, swords, spears, and horse fittings (now missing){{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992|qq=}}. * 1899 (Meiji 32): The burial chamber was identified{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. * 1901 (Meiji 34): Soil extraction revealed a small stone burial chamber and grave goods{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. * 1988 (Showa 63): Trial excavation conducted by the Yodoe Town Board of Education (report published in 1990){{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992|qq=}}. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Chojaganaru Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Burial Chamber - Main Chamber (back wall)|qq=}}]] The burial facility consists of a left single-sleeve horizontal stone chamber (some sources describe it as a western-leaning double-sleeve type{{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}) opening to the southeast{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. Key dimensions are as follows{{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}: * Total chamber length: 10.3 meters * Main chamber (gen-shitsu): Length 5.7 meters, width 2.6 meters, height 2.6 meters The chamber is constructed using quarried and natural stones in wide and narrow stacking styles{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. Reports mention iron swords and spearheads among the grave goods, though their current whereabouts are unknown{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. North of the stone chamber, a small box-type sarcophagus was discovered in 1901. This contained human remains, a gilt bronze crown, a gilt bronze triple-ring pommel, three gilt bronze magatama (curved beads), and six bronze bells{{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}. These items are preserved at {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|qid=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}{{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992|qq=}}. <gallery> File:Chojaganaru Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards entrance) File:Chojaganaru Kofun, sendou.jpg|Passage </gallery> == Notes == {{reflist|qq=}} == References == {{small|Sources used for this article|qq=}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukōyama Kofun Cluster Pamphlet]|qq=}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster Pamphlet|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Chōjaganaru Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 32: Tottori Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takaki Obara|year=1989|chapter=Chōjaganaru Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Chōjaganaru Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Fumiaki Iwata|year=2002|chapter=Mukōyama Kofun Cluster|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550948|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Cluster (Zoku Kofun)|2002|qq=}}|qq=}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional references not cited in this article|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=Mukōyama Kofun Cluster - A Study of Mukōyama, Binzan, and Ishiumadani Kofuns (Yodoe Town Cultural Property Excavation Report, Vol. 17)|publisher=Yodoe Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=|qq=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Chojaganaru Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukoyama Kofun Cluster]]
